criteria.label: Write a simple property test with `@given` + `strategies`.
Identify candidate properties: round-trip, idempotence, invariants, oracle equivalence.
Pin every shrunk falsifying input as an `@example(...)` so it's tested deterministically every run.
Constrain strategies to the real input domain (no surrogates if your code doesn't accept them).
Tune `@settings(max_examples, deadline, derandomize)` per property's importance and CI budget.
criteria.example: `@settings(max_examples=500, deadline=2_000, derandomize=True)` on a critical property.

criteria.label: Use stateful (`RuleBasedStateMachine`) tests for caches, indices, queues and other stateful systems.
criteria.example: 30-line state machine with `@rule add/remove` and `@invariant always_consistent` — finds ordering bugs hand-written tests can't.
